Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product in the inventory.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

sku.description: The Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) of the product.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the product is currently active and available for sale.

InventoryMovement.title: InventoryMovement
InventoryMovement.description: Represents a movement of inventory for a specific product.

id.description: Unique identifier for the inventory movement.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product that was moved (positive for increase, negative for decrease).

movementDate.description: The date and time when the inventory movement occurred.

description.description: An explanation of why the movement was made.

id.description: Unique identifier for the sale transaction.

saleDate.description: The date and time when the sale occurred.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the sale.

paymentMethod.description: The method of payment used for the sale (e.g., 'cash', 'credit card').

saleItems.description: A list of sale items for the sale

SaleItem.title: SaleItem
SaleItem.description: Represents an item included in a sale transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the sale item.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product sold in this sale item.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time of the sale.

discount.description: The discount applied to this sale item, if any.

Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Represents a purchase transaction to add new products to the store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the purchase transaction.

purchaseDate.description: The date and time when the purchase was made.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the purchase.

purchaseItems.description: A list of purchase items for the purchase

PurchaseItem.title: PurchaseItem
PurchaseItem.description: Represents an item included in a purchase transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the purchase item.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product purchased in this purchase item.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time of the purchase.

firstName.description: The first name of the customer.

lastName.description: The last name of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the customer.

contactName.description: The name of the contact person at the supplier.

email.description: The email address of the supplier.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the supplier.

TaxType.title: TaxType
TaxType.description: Represents a type of tax that can be applied to a product.

id.description: Unique identifier for the tax type.

name.description: The name of the tax type (e.g., 'VAT', 'Sales Tax').

rate.description: The tax rate as a decimal (e.g., 0.10 for 10%).

Configuration.title: Configuration
Configuration.description: Represents configuration settings for the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the configuration settings.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the business.

securityPin.description: The security PIN required to access the application.

currency.description: The currency used by the business (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

params.name: inventoryMovementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inventory movement.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sale.

definition.description: Stores individual sale items related to a specific sale.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sale.

params.name: saleItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sale item.

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the purchase.

definition.description: Stores individual purchase items related to a specific purchase.

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the purchase.

params.name: purchaseItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the purchase item.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

params.name: supplierId
params.description: The unique identifier for the supplier.

params.name: taxTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tax type.

params.name: configurationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the configuration settings.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the inventory and point-of-sale system described. It prioritizes security, scalability, and ease of debugging. Key design decisions include:

*   **Authorization Independence:** The structure avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies to ensure atomic operations and simplified security rules.
*   **Structural Segregation:** Data with different access requirements (e.g., public vs. private) is stored in separate collections.
*   **Access Modeling:** Standardized access patterns are used, primarily path-based ownership.

Specifically:

*   `/products`: Stores product information.
*   `/inventoryMovements/{inventoryMovementId}`: Stores inventory movement records, with `productId` for relation to products.
*   `/sales/{saleId}`: Stores sales transactions, with `customerId` for relation to customers and `saleItems` as an array for storing SaleItems.
*   `/sales/{saleId}/saleItems/{saleItemId}`: Stores individual sale items related to a specific sale.
*   `/purchases/{purchaseId}`: Stores purchase transactions, with `supplierId` for relation to suppliers and `purchaseItems` as an array for storing PurchaseItems.
*   `/purchases/{purchaseId}/purchaseItems/{purchaseItemId}`: Stores individual purchase items related to a specific purchase.
*   `/customers`: Stores customer information.
*   `/suppliers`: Stores supplier information.
*   `/taxTypes`: Stores tax type information.
*   `/configuration/{configurationId}`: Stores business configuration data.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters) are supported by segregating data based on its security requirements. Path-based ownership ensures secure list operations for user-owned data. The membership map approach facilitates secure collaborative access where needed.
